title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They will announce the results at the end of the week.
questions.afterGap: at the end of the week.

questions.firstSentence: ‘You should take more exercise,’ the doctor told me.
The only way to get there on time is to take a taxi.
questions.beforeGap: You won't get there on time

questions.firstSentence: I'm certain he was very tired after the long journey.
questions.afterGap: very tired after the long journey.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for positive deduction.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't realize my mistake until I saw the results.
questions.beforeGap: Only when I saw the results

questions.firstSentence: It was so hot that we decided to stay indoors.
questions.afterGap: that we decided to stay indoors.
questions.explanation: Tests 'such' with adjective and noun for emphasis.
